## What Changed

- Added the publish-ready npm MCP package `@nangman-infra/touch-browser-mcp` as the primary local-host MCP distribution path
- Aligned README, MCP docs, operations docs, and example configs around the public docs / research web MCP surface
- Clarified the MCP contract so search engine selection stays automatic and headed browsing remains outside the MCP surface
- Split the GitHub Actions quality gate into observable lint/fmt/typecheck/clippy/contracts/rust/adapter/evals steps so CI failures no longer collapse into a single opaque step
- Updated architecture checks and DDD completion criteria to enforce the new CI observability contract

## User Impact

- Local MCP hosts can use `npx -y @nangman-infra/touch-browser-mcp` directly instead of requiring a separate manual runtime install first
- The public MCP surface now reads more like an evidence workflow and less like a browser debugging surface
- Future CI failures should be diagnosable from the workflow step name alone instead of requiring a full rerun to discover which sub-gate failed

## Notes

- This release follows `v0.1.7`
- The standalone GitHub Release bundle remains the fallback/offline/operator channel
- The npm MCP package version is now aligned with this release tag at `0.1.8`
